questions.prompt_html:
A closed rectangular container of volume 96 cubic metres is to be made from three different materials. 
The top and the bottom of the container are to be made from a material that costs $4 per square metre, two parallel sides (say left and right) are to be made from a material that costs $3 per square metre, and the other two parallel sides (front and back) are to be made from a material that costs $1 per square metre.
Let x and y be the dimensions of the base of the container and z be its height in metres.
(i)Express the total cost of the container (in dollars) as a function of x and y.
(ii)Find dimensions of the most economical container and how much it costs.
questions.prompt_text: A closed rectangular container of volume 96 cubic metres is to be made from three different materials. The top and the bottom of the container are to be made from a material that costs $4 per square metre, two parallel sides (say left and right) are to be made from a material that costs $3 per square metre, and the other two parallel sides (front and back) are to be made from a material that costs $1 per square metre. Let x and y be the dimensions of the base of the container and z be its height in metres. (i) Express the total cost of the container (in dollars) as a function of x and y. (ii) Find dimensions of the most economical container and how much it costs.
questions.solution_html:
(i)The cost function C(x,y) = 8xy +  +  dollars.
Another possible correct answer is C(x,y) = 8xy +  +  dollars.
(ii)Dimensions are 6 m by 2 m by 8 m; cost is $288
questions.solution_text: (i) The cost function C(x,y) = 8xy + (576/x) + (192/y) dollars. Another possible correct answer is C(x,y) = 8xy + (192/x) + (576/y) dollars. (ii) Dimensions are 6 m by 2 m by 8 m; cost is $288
